title: The Reason behind Rifi ' b. Layth's Revolt
description: According to what has been mentioned to us, the reason for that was that Yalaya b. al-Ash'ath b. Yabyi al-Till married one of the daughters, who was famed for her richness,8 92 of his paternal uncle Abu al-Nu'min. YaIyi stayed in the City of Peace, and left her at 891. Possibly the grandson of the last Umayyad governor of Khurasan. His father was presumably the Layth "mawU of the Commander of the Faithful " sent in alMansur's reign as an envoy to the lbrkish ruler of Farghinah. See Ya'gnbi, Ta'rikh, II, 465-6j Barthold, Turkestan, Sol.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 30
